evals.name: startup-state-file-schema
evals.prompt: Run: node plugins/code-review/skills/code-review-loop/scripts/reviewer.js 'review staged changes for quality' --max-iterations 3 --model gpt-5.4 --mode claude in the project root. Capture stdout and the contents of .claude/code-review.local.md. If Copilot CLI is unavailable, kill the command after the banner is printed (banner is written before copilot.js is spawned).
evals.expected_output: Startup banner printed with 'Code Review Loop activated'. .claude/code-review.local.md exists with YAML frontmatter containing active:true, iteration:1, max_iterations:3, completion_promise:APPROVAL, model:'gpt-5.4', mode:'claude', started_at (ISO), and a body containing the original prompt.

assertions.name: startup_banner_printed
assertions.description: stdout contains 'Code Review Loop activated in this session!'

assertions.name: state_file_created
assertions.description: .claude/code-review.local.md exists after running the script

assertions.name: state_active_true
assertions.description: frontmatter contains 'active: true'

assertions.name: state_iteration_one
assertions.description: frontmatter contains 'iteration: 1'

assertions.name: state_max_iterations_three
assertions.description: frontmatter contains 'max_iterations: 3'

assertions.name: state_completion_promise_approval
assertions.description: frontmatter contains 'completion_promise: "APPROVAL"'

assertions.name: state_model_persisted
assertions.description: frontmatter contains 'model: "gpt-5.4"' so the stop hook can re-invoke the same reviewer

assertions.name: state_mode_persisted
assertions.description: frontmatter contains 'mode: "claude"'

assertions.name: prompt_in_body
assertions.description: state file body contains 'review staged changes for quality'

evals.name: startup-banner-role-and-approval-ownership
evals.prompt: Run: node plugins/code-review/skills/code-review-loop/scripts/reviewer.js 'review staged changes' --max-iterations 1 --model gpt-5.4 --mode claude and capture stdout. Verify the banner establishes writer/reviewer separation AND declares the approval token as reviewer-exclusive.
evals.expected_output: Banner contains the 'ROLE SEPARATION — STRICT' block, forbids self-review, AND explicitly forbids the writer session from emitting <promise>APPROVAL</promise> (declares it reviewer-exclusive and reports that the stop hook inspects the reviewer's persisted report, not the writer's messages).

assertions.name: banner_role_separation_header
assertions.description: stdout contains 'ROLE SEPARATION — STRICT'

assertions.name: banner_forbids_self_review
assertions.description: stdout contains 'MUST NOT conduct the code review yourself'

assertions.name: banner_forbids_writer_emitting_approval
assertions.description: stdout contains a clause forbidding the writer from emitting the approval token (e.g. 'YOU MUST NOT emit <promise>APPROVAL</promise>' or 'reserved for the Copilot reviewer exclusively')

assertions.name: banner_explains_reviewer_owns_terminator
assertions.description: stdout states the loop terminates only when the REVIEWER\'s report ends with the literal token

assertions.name: banner_mentions_persisted_report
assertions.description: stdout references that the reviewer's report is persisted, or that the stop hook inspects the report rather than the writer's messages

evals.name: copilot-js-injects-approval-protocol
evals.prompt: Load plugins/code-review/skills/code-review-loop/scripts/copilot.js as a module and assert that it exports `wrapReviewerPrompt`. Call `wrapReviewerPrompt('REVIEW THIS DIFF')` and inspect the returned string. This is the exact prompt body forwarded to Copilot CLI.
evals.expected_output: The wrapped prompt starts with the user's literal prompt ('REVIEW THIS DIFF') and ends with an approval protocol that instructs Copilot to emit `<promise>APPROVAL</promise>` alone on the final line if and only if the iteration passes, and to backtick-quote the token in any other mention.

assertions.name: user_prompt_preserved_at_start
assertions.description: wrapReviewerPrompt('REVIEW THIS DIFF') returns a string that begins with 'REVIEW THIS DIFF'

assertions.name: protocol_mentions_loop_termination
assertions.description: wrapped prompt contains the phrase 'LOOP TERMINATION PROTOCOL'

assertions.name: protocol_instructs_terminator_only_on_pass
assertions.description: wrapped prompt instructs Copilot to emit <promise>APPROVAL</promise> 'if and only if' (or equivalent hard conditional) the iteration passes review

assertions.name: protocol_instructs_final_line_alone
assertions.description: wrapped prompt says the token must be on the FINAL line of the output with nothing else on that line

assertions.name: protocol_instructs_backtick_quote_when_discussing
assertions.description: wrapped prompt instructs Copilot to wrap the token in backticks when mentioning it in prose or examples

assertions.name: protocol_reserves_tag_for_reviewer
assertions.description: wrapped prompt states the writer/fixer session will NOT emit the tag — only the reviewer terminates the loop

evals.name: iteration1-persists-reviewer-report-to-disk
evals.prompt: Run reviewer.js with any prompt and --model <any model> --mode claude. Intercept or replace copilot.js with a stub that prints a known reviewer-style report to stdout (e.g., 'Mock review\nImportant: bar\n'). Verify that after reviewer.js exits, .claude/code-review.last-report.md exists and contains the captured stdout.
evals.expected_output: .claude/code-review.last-report.md exists after iteration 1, contains the reviewer's full stdout verbatim (streamed output was both echoed to terminal AND persisted).

assertions.name: report_file_created_after_iteration1
assertions.description: .claude/code-review.last-report.md exists after reviewer.js exits

assertions.name: report_file_content_matches_reviewer_stdout
assertions.description: report file contents match the captured Copilot stdout byte-for-byte (no re-formatting)

assertions.name: reviewer_stdout_also_streamed_to_terminal
assertions.description: reviewer.js still prints the reviewer's output to its own stdout (tee behaviour — the writer can still see the report in the conversation)

evals.name: stop-hook-approval-via-report-file
evals.prompt: Test: stop hook approves the loop ONLY when the reviewer's persisted report ends with the terminator on its own final non-empty line. Steps: (1) Create a throwaway git repo with a committed file. (2) Create .claude/code-review.local.md (active, iteration:1, max_iterations:3, model:'gpt-5.4', mode:'claude'). (3) Create .claude/code-review.last-report.md whose final non-empty line is exactly '<promise>APPROVAL</promise>'. (4) Run: echo '{"transcript_path":"","cwd":"<repo>","last_assistant_message":"anything — should be ignored"}' | node plugins/code-review/scripts/session-stop.js claude.
evals.expected_output: stdout contains '✅ Code review loop: Reviewer issued APPROVAL in its latest report.' Both .claude/code-review.local.md and .claude/code-review.last-report.md are deleted. Exit code 0. No 'decision' JSON emitted.

assertions.name: approval_message_emitted
assertions.description: stdout contains '✅ Code review loop: Reviewer issued APPROVAL in its latest report.'

assertions.name: state_file_cleared_on_approval
assertions.description: .claude/code-review.local.md is deleted

assertions.name: report_file_cleared_on_approval
assertions.description: .claude/code-review.last-report.md is deleted

assertions.name: no_block_decision_on_approval
assertions.description: stdout does not contain '"decision":'

assertions.name: approval_source_is_report_file
assertions.description: approval is granted regardless of last_assistant_message contents — the writer's message is not consulted (i.e. the hook still approves even when last_assistant_message is empty or contradictory)

evals.name: stop-hook-ignores-writer-emitted-approval-token
evals.prompt: Regression test for the ambiguity-prose bug. Test: the writer cannot end the loop by emitting the terminator themselves. Setup same as test 5 but: (a) create .claude/code-review.last-report.md WITHOUT the terminator (e.g. 'Important: fix foo()'), and (b) set last_assistant_message to a writer message that quotes '<promise>APPROVAL</promise>'. Also make a working-tree change so the block path can fire. Run the hook.
evals.expected_output: The hook does NOT approve. It either emits a block decision (if Copilot is available and produces a non-approving report) or blocks with a fallback reason (if Copilot fails). It never emits '✅ Code review loop: Reviewer issued APPROVAL'. The state file is NOT deleted by the approval path.

assertions.name: no_false_approval_from_writer_message
assertions.description: stdout does NOT contain '✅ Code review loop: Reviewer issued APPROVAL'

assertions.name: block_path_or_fallback_fires
assertions.description: stdout contains a block decision (JSON with decision:block) OR the state file remains and iteration was incremented

assertions.name: state_file_not_cleared_by_approval_path
assertions.description: .claude/code-review.local.md was not deleted by the approval branch (it may be overwritten by the block branch with a new iteration)

evals.name: stop-hook-pattern-rejects-tag-not-on-final-line
evals.prompt: Verify the strict last-line-alone matcher. Setup: state file present, working tree has uncommitted changes. Create .claude/code-review.last-report.md with content that contains '<promise>APPROVAL</promise>' in the middle of prose AND on the final line but WITH trailing prose after it. Run the stop hook.
evals.expected_output: The hook does NOT approve. Proceeds to block path. The pattern must only match when the terminator is the final non-empty line alone.

assertions.name: tag_in_prose_middle_rejected
assertions.description: Given a report whose middle line contains <promise>APPROVAL</promise> inside a sentence, the hook does not emit the approval message

assertions.name: tag_with_trailing_prose_rejected
assertions.description: Given a report whose final non-empty line is NOT the token (but an earlier line is), the hook does not emit the approval message

assertions.name: tag_in_backticks_rejected
assertions.description: Given a report whose final non-empty line is e.g. '`<promise>APPROVAL</promise>`' with backticks, the hook does not emit the approval message

evals.name: stop-hook-block-rolls-state-and-persists-new-report
evals.prompt: Test: on iteration 2+, the stop hook (i) rolls state, (ii) re-invokes Copilot with the persisted model, (iii) writes the new report to .claude/code-review.last-report.md (or clears it on failure so stale tags cannot carry over). Setup: repo with diff + state file (iteration:1, model:'gpt-5.4') + existing report file. Run the hook.
evals.expected_output: stdout contains a JSON block decision. State file iteration becomes 2 with base_revision and head_sha populated (40-hex SHAs). The report file now reflects the NEW reviewer run (content replaced) OR was deleted if the invocation failed — the old report content must NOT remain unchanged on disk.

assertions.name: block_decision_emitted
assertions.description: stdout parses as JSON and has decision === 'block'

assertions.name: iteration_incremented_to_2
assertions.description: state file frontmatter shows iteration:2

assertions.name: base_and_head_are_sha
assertions.description: base_revision and head_sha are 40-char hex strings

assertions.name: report_file_overwritten_or_cleared
assertions.description: After the hook runs, .claude/code-review.last-report.md either contains the new reviewer output (different from the pre-existing stub content) OR has been deleted (Copilot invocation failed). It must NOT retain stale pre-existing content.

assertions.name: system_message_announces_persistence_state
assertions.description: decision.systemMessage contains 'Reviewer: Copilot report persisted' or 'Reviewer: invocation failed — report cleared'

evals.name: stop-hook-block-reason-forbids-self-review-and-self-approval
evals.prompt: Inspect the decision.reason emitted on the block path (setup same as test 8). Regardless of Copilot availability, the reason must (a) forbid self-review by the writer, and (b) forbid the writer from emitting the approval token.
evals.expected_output: decision.reason contains both prohibitions: 'DO NOT conduct your own review' or 'Do NOT review the diff yourself', AND 'DO NOT emit `<promise>APPROVAL</promise>`' or 'Do NOT emit the approval token'.

assertions.name: reason_forbids_self_review
assertions.description: decision.reason contains 'DO NOT conduct your own review' or 'Do NOT review the diff yourself' or 'Do NOT analyze the diff yourself'

assertions.name: reason_forbids_writer_emitting_token
assertions.description: decision.reason contains 'DO NOT emit `<promise>APPROVAL</promise>`' or 'Do NOT emit the approval token'

assertions.name: reason_mentions_reviewer_owns_termination
assertions.description: decision.reason contains a phrase like 'the stop hook inspects the persisted reviewer report', 'reserved for the reviewer', or 'Only the reviewer can terminate the loop'

assertions.name: reason_never_instructs_writer_to_judge
assertions.description: decision.reason does NOT contain imperatives telling the writer to judge approval, e.g. 'Output <promise>APPROVAL</promise> ONLY if ...' addressed to the writer session

evals.name: stop-hook-uses-persisted-model
evals.prompt: Verify the stop hook spawns copilot.js with the model from state (not a hard-coded default). Setup as test 8 but with state.model = 'probe-model-xyz'. Intercept the copilot.js invocation (shim copilot.js to log argv, or inspect stderr from a failing invocation which echoes the model name).
evals.expected_output: copilot.js receives '--model probe-model-xyz'. On failure, the fallback decision.reason references that same model string.

assertions.name: copilot_invoked_with_persisted_model
assertions.description: The spawned copilot.js process is given --model probe-model-xyz

assertions.name: fallback_reason_references_probe_model
assertions.description: If invocation fails, decision.reason contains '--model probe-model-xyz'

evals.name: stop-hook-max-iterations-suspends-loop-preserving-state
evals.prompt: Verify the max-iterations exit path SUSPENDS the loop without clearing state. Setup: .claude/code-review.local.md with iteration:3, max_iterations:3, active:true, model/mode set, and a .claude/code-review.last-report.md present (without terminator). Run: echo '{"transcript_path":"","cwd":"<repo>","last_assistant_message":"..."}' | node plugins/code-review/scripts/session-stop.js claude.
evals.expected_output: stdout contains 'max iterations (3) reached at iteration 3. Loop suspended; state preserved.' and instructions mentioning /continue-loop AND /cancel-review. Both state and report files REMAIN on disk. Exit code 0. Copilot is NOT invoked. Only a confirmed APPROVAL (id:5) is allowed to clear state.

assertions.name: suspension_message_with_limits
assertions.description: stdout contains 'max iterations (3) reached at iteration 3' and 'Loop suspended; state preserved.'

assertions.name: message_mentions_cancel_review
assertions.description: stdout contains '/cancel-review' as the documented way to discard state

assertions.name: message_mentions_continue_loop
assertions.description: stdout contains '/continue-loop' and indicates --max-iterations N is the way to raise the cap

assertions.name: state_file_preserved
assertions.description: .claude/code-review.local.md still exists after the hook runs (state management rule: only APPROVAL clears state)

assertions.name: report_file_preserved
assertions.description: .claude/code-review.last-report.md still exists after the hook runs

assertions.name: no_block_decision
assertions.description: stdout does not contain '"decision":' (the hook allows exit; user decides next action)

assertions.name: no_copilot_invocation
assertions.description: copilot.js is not spawned on the max-iterations exit path

evals.name: stop-hook-no-changes-preserves-state-and-does-not-bump-iteration
evals.prompt: Verify the 'no changes detected' path preserves state and does NOT bump iteration. Setup: throwaway git repo, single committed file, clean working tree, HEAD unchanged since initial_head. .claude/code-review.local.md with iteration:1, max_iterations:5, initial_head set to HEAD. .claude/code-review.last-report.md present (without terminator). Run the stop hook.
evals.expected_output: stderr contains a message like 'no changes detected since iteration 1. Address the reviewer's findings ...'. State file and report file both remain on disk. Iteration in state remains 1 (NOT bumped). Exit code 0. No block decision emitted. Copilot is NOT invoked (no diff to review).

assertions.name: no_changes_warning_emitted
assertions.description: stderr contains 'no changes detected since iteration 1'

assertions.name: state_preserved_on_no_changes
assertions.description: .claude/code-review.local.md still exists

assertions.name: report_preserved_on_no_changes
assertions.description: .claude/code-review.last-report.md still exists (the previous review verdict is still authoritative)

assertions.name: iteration_not_bumped_on_no_changes
assertions.description: state file iteration remains 1 — this path must not consume an iteration slot

assertions.name: no_block_decision_on_no_changes
assertions.description: stdout does not contain '"decision":'

assertions.name: no_copilot_invocation_on_no_changes
assertions.description: copilot.js is not spawned because there is nothing new to review

evals.name: stop-hook-corrupt-state-warns-without-auto-delete
evals.prompt: Verify that a corrupt or unparseable state file is NOT auto-deleted. Setup: write garbage to .claude/code-review.local.md whose frontmatter fails to parse (e.g. unterminated YAML or binary bytes). Run the stop hook.
evals.expected_output: stderr contains a warning like 'failed to parse ... corrupt — inspect it manually or run /cancel-review to discard it.' Exit code 0. The corrupt state file REMAINS on disk — the user must decide whether to fix or discard. Only APPROVAL clears state.

assertions.name: corrupt_state_warning_emitted
assertions.description: stderr contains 'corrupt' and '/cancel-review'

assertions.name: corrupt_state_file_preserved
assertions.description: .claude/code-review.local.md is NOT deleted even when unparseable (state management rule)

assertions.name: no_block_decision_on_corrupt_state
assertions.description: stdout does not contain '"decision":'

evals.name: startup-default-max-iterations-is-three
evals.prompt: Run reviewer.js WITHOUT a --max-iterations flag: node plugins/code-review/skills/code-review-loop/scripts/reviewer.js 'review staged changes' --model gpt-5.4 --mode claude. Inspect the resulting state file.
evals.expected_output: The frontmatter contains 'max_iterations: 3'. The help text printed by 'reviewer.js --help' advertises 'default: 3'. Passing '--max-iterations 7' overrides to 7; passing '--max-iterations 0' records 0 (explicit unlimited).

assertions.name: state_default_max_iterations_three
assertions.description: When --max-iterations is omitted, .claude/code-review.local.md contains 'max_iterations: 3'

assertions.name: help_advertises_default_three
assertions.description: reviewer.js --help stdout contains 'default: 3' on the --max-iterations line

assertions.name: max_iterations_override_honoured
assertions.description: Passing '--max-iterations 7' results in 'max_iterations: 7' in state

assertions.name: explicit_zero_means_unlimited
assertions.description: Passing '--max-iterations 0' records 'max_iterations: 0' (unlimited opt-in still available)

evals.name: reviewer-prompt-excludes-plugin-state-files
evals.prompt: Load copilot.js as a module and call buildExclusionClause(). Verify it explicitly names each plugin state path in both claude and copilot modes, and instructs the reviewer to silently skip them. Also verify that both the iteration-1 path (reviewer.js → copilot.js) and the iteration-2+ path (session-stop.js → copilot.js) concatenate this clause into the prompt that is handed to the Copilot CLI.
evals.expected_output: buildExclusionClause() returns a string containing each of '.claude/code-review.local.md', '.claude/code-review.last-report.md', '.copilot/code-review.local.md', '.copilot/code-review.last-report.md', plus the word 'silently' (telling the reviewer to ignore diffs to these files without mentioning them). Both reviewer.js and session-stop.js include this clause in the prompt argv of the spawned copilot.js.

assertions.name: exclusion_lists_claude_state
assertions.description: clause contains '.claude/code-review.local.md' and '.claude/code-review.last-report.md'

assertions.name: exclusion_lists_copilot_state
assertions.description: clause contains '.copilot/code-review.local.md' and '.copilot/code-review.last-report.md'

assertions.name: exclusion_instructs_silently_skip
assertions.description: clause contains the word 'silently' and 'Do NOT list them as findings'

assertions.name: iteration1_prompt_includes_exclusion
assertions.description: The prompt argv passed from reviewer.js to copilot.js contains 'REVIEW SCOPE — EXCLUSIONS'

assertions.name: iteration2plus_prompt_includes_exclusion
assertions.description: The prompt passed from session-stop.js to copilot.js (observable when Copilot fails and stderr echoes the argv) contains 'REVIEW SCOPE — EXCLUSIONS'

evals.name: reviewer-prompt-injects-emotional-stimuli-near-cap
evals.prompt: Verify buildLoopContextSuffix(currentIteration, maxIterations) from copilot.js behaves as follows. Unlimited modes (maxIterations <= 0, NaN, undefined) → empty string. Far-from-cap (e.g. iter=1, max=3) → empty string. Near-final (remaining = 1, e.g. iter=2, max=3) → non-empty, mentions 'Only 1 iteration remains' and 'deep breath'. Final (remaining = 0, e.g. iter=3, max=3) → non-empty, mentions 'FINAL iteration' and 'No further retries'. Also verify the raw <promise>APPROVAL</promise> token inside the suffix is backtick-quoted so it cannot accidentally match the terminator pattern. Then verify session-stop.js block path composes this suffix into the reviewer prompt when near the cap.
evals.expected_output: Empty suffix for unlimited/far-from-cap. Non-empty suffix with 'deep breath', 'do your best', and 'Please read carefully' in the final 1–2 iterations. Final iteration suffix additionally contains 'FINAL' and 'No further retries'. The literal approval token inside the suffix is enclosed in backticks: `<promise>APPROVAL</promise>`. When a loop with max_iterations=2 runs its second iteration, the session-stop.js argv to copilot.js contains 'ITERATION CONTEXT — PLEASE READ CAREFULLY'.

assertions.name: unlimited_returns_empty
assertions.description: buildLoopContextSuffix(1, 0), (1, -1), (1, undefined) all return ''

assertions.name: far_from_cap_returns_empty
assertions.description: buildLoopContextSuffix(1, 10) returns '' (remaining = 9)

assertions.name: near_final_contains_stimulus
assertions.description: buildLoopContextSuffix(2, 3) contains 'Only 1 iteration remains', 'deep breath', 'do your best'

assertions.name: final_iteration_contains_no_retries
assertions.description: buildLoopContextSuffix(3, 3) contains 'FINAL iteration' and 'No further retries'

assertions.name: tag_is_backtick_quoted_in_stimulus
assertions.description: Any suffix emitted by buildLoopContextSuffix that mentions the approval token does so with surrounding backticks (so the terminator matcher cannot match lines inside the instruction)

assertions.name: session_stop_injects_stimulus_near_cap
assertions.description: Running session-stop.js with state iteration:1 and max_iterations:2 and a dirty working tree causes the copilot.js argv to include 'ITERATION CONTEXT — PLEASE READ CAREFULLY'

assertions.name: session_stop_does_not_inject_far_from_cap
assertions.description: Running session-stop.js with state iteration:1 and max_iterations:10 and a dirty working tree does NOT inject 'ITERATION CONTEXT' into the copilot.js argv

evals.name: stop-hook-only-approval-clears-state
evals.prompt: Summary invariant test. Run the stop hook under each of these scenarios in sequence and record whether .claude/code-review.local.md survives: (A) APPROVAL in report; (B) max_iterations reached; (C) no-changes; (D) unresolved base_revision; (E) block path with successful Copilot; (F) block path with failed Copilot. In every case except (A), the state file must remain on disk.
evals.expected_output: State file clearance matrix: A=cleared; B=preserved; C=preserved; D=preserved; E=preserved (iteration bumped); F=preserved (iteration bumped). The state file is cleared by the stop hook in one and only one scenario: reviewer APPROVAL in the persisted report.

assertions.name: approval_is_the_only_path_that_clears_state
assertions.description: Across scenarios A–F, exactly one (A = APPROVAL) results in .claude/code-review.local.md being deleted by the stop hook

assertions.name: non_approval_paths_preserve_state
assertions.description: Scenarios B, C, D, E, F all leave .claude/code-review.local.md on disk

evals.name: writer-session-never-emits-approval-token
evals.prompt: Behavioral eval (transcript audit). Run the skill: /code-review-loop Review src/example.ts for quality --max-iterations 3. Assume Copilot CLI is installed and produces a normal multi-iteration review-fix sequence. Audit EVERY writer-session assistant message across all iterations.
evals.expected_output: In every writer assistant message, the exact string '<promise>APPROVAL</promise>' never appears — not even inside code blocks, quotations, or paraphrases. The writer also does not produce its own Critical/Important/Suggestion/Nit findings sections. The loop still terminates correctly (either via reviewer-emitted approval in the persisted report or via max-iterations).

assertions.name: writer_never_emits_approval_tag
assertions.description: No writer-role assistant message across the transcript contains the substring '<promise>APPROVAL</promise>' in any form (raw, backticked, or inside a code block)

assertions.name: no_writer_findings_sections
assertions.description: No writer message contains a heading line matching /^#+\s*(Critical|Important|Suggestion|Nit)\s*$/ or a 'My review:' / 'I reviewed' style self-review section

assertions.name: no_exploratory_reads_for_review
assertions.description: No writer Read/Grep/Glob tool call is issued for the purpose of forming its own review (file access driven by a reviewer-listed fix is allowed)

assertions.name: loop_terminates_via_reviewer_report_or_max_iterations
assertions.description: The loop ends either (a) because the persisted .claude/code-review.last-report.md ended with the terminator, or (b) because max-iterations was reached. Termination via writer-emitted tag is impossible by design.

evals.name: cancel-review-clears-state-and-report
evals.prompt: Invoke /cancel-review when both .claude/code-review.local.md and .claude/code-review.last-report.md exist.
evals.expected_output: Both files are removed. The command reports 'Cancelled code review loop (was at iteration N)' using the iteration from state.

assertions.name: state_file_removed
assertions.description: .claude/code-review.local.md no longer exists

assertions.name: report_file_removed
assertions.description: .claude/code-review.last-report.md no longer exists

assertions.name: cancellation_message_with_iteration
assertions.description: Output contains 'Cancelled code review loop (was at iteration' followed by the numeric iteration

evals.name: continue-loop-rejects-without-active-state
evals.prompt: Run: node plugins/code-review/skills/code-review-loop/scripts/continue.js --max-iterations 5 in a fresh git repo with no .claude/code-review.local.md. Capture stdout + stderr + exit code and verify the decision matrix: no active loop path rejects cleanly without side effects.
evals.expected_output: Exit code 1. stderr contains 'No active code review loop found'. No state file is created. Copilot is NOT invoked.

assertions.name: no_active_loop_message
assertions.description: stderr contains 'No active code review loop found'

assertions.name: exit_code_one
assertions.description: exit code is 1

assertions.name: no_state_file_created
assertions.description: no .claude/code-review.local.md is created by continue.js

assertions.name: no_copilot_invocation
assertions.description: copilot.js is not spawned

evals.name: continue-loop-rejects-at-cap-without-flag
evals.prompt: Setup: git repo with a committed file and .claude/code-review.local.md containing iteration:3, max_iterations:3, active:true, model:'gpt-5.4', mode:'claude'. Run: node plugins/code-review/skills/code-review-loop/scripts/continue.js (no flags).
evals.expected_output: Exit code 1. stderr contains 'Loop is at its cap (iteration 3 / max 3)' and 'Pass --max-iterations'. State file iteration and max_iterations remain 3. Copilot is NOT invoked.

assertions.name: at_cap_message
assertions.description: stderr contains 'at its cap' and 'Pass --max-iterations'

assertions.name: exit_code_one
assertions.description: exit code is 1

assertions.name: state_iteration_unchanged
assertions.description: .claude/code-review.local.md still has iteration: 3

assertions.name: state_max_unchanged
assertions.description: .claude/code-review.local.md still has max_iterations: 3

assertions.name: no_copilot_invocation
assertions.description: copilot.js is not spawned

evals.name: continue-loop-raises-cap-and-runs-reviewer
evals.prompt: Setup: git repo with a committed file + dirty working tree (so git stash create yields a snapshot). .claude/code-review.local.md with iteration:3, max_iterations:3, active:true, model:'gpt-5.4', mode:'claude', head_sha set to HEAD. Intercept copilot.js with a shim that prints 'SHIM-REVIEW\nImportant: foo\n'. Run: node plugins/code-review/skills/code-review-loop/scripts/continue.js --max-iterations 5.
evals.expected_output: Exit code 0. stdout contains the continue banner ('Code Review Loop continued', 'Iteration: 4', 'ROLE SEPARATION'), followed by the reviewer report including 'SHIM-REVIEW' and 'Important: foo'. State updates atomically: iteration becomes 4, max_iterations becomes 5, base_revision and head_sha are 40-hex SHAs. .claude/code-review.last-report.md contains the shim stdout. copilot.js is invoked exactly once with --model gpt-5.4 and a prompt that includes the range 'base..head' and 'REVIEW SCOPE — EXCLUSIONS'.

assertions.name: banner_continued_line
assertions.description: stdout contains 'Code Review Loop continued'

assertions.name: banner_iteration_next
assertions.description: stdout contains 'Iteration: 4'

assertions.name: banner_role_separation
assertions.description: stdout contains 'ROLE SEPARATION'

assertions.name: reviewer_report_streamed
assertions.description: stdout contains the reviewer shim output (e.g. 'SHIM-REVIEW', 'Important: foo')

assertions.name: state_iteration_incremented
assertions.description: .claude/code-review.local.md shows iteration: 4

assertions.name: state_max_raised
assertions.description: .claude/code-review.local.md shows max_iterations: 5

assertions.name: state_head_and_base_sha
assertions.description: base_revision and head_sha in state are 40-char hex SHAs

assertions.name: report_persisted
assertions.description: .claude/code-review.last-report.md contains the shim reviewer output

assertions.name: copilot_invoked_with_model
assertions.description: copilot.js was spawned once with '--model gpt-5.4'

assertions.name: copilot_prompt_has_range
assertions.description: the prompt passed to copilot.js contains a 'sha..sha' git range

assertions.name: copilot_prompt_has_exclusion
assertions.description: the prompt passed to copilot.js contains 'REVIEW SCOPE — EXCLUSIONS'

evals.name: continue-loop-rejects-without-new-diff
evals.prompt: Setup: git repo with a committed file, clean working tree, HEAD unchanged. .claude/code-review.local.md with iteration:3, max_iterations:3, head_sha = current HEAD. Run: node plugins/code-review/skills/code-review-loop/scripts/continue.js --max-iterations 5.
evals.expected_output: Exit code 1. stderr contains 'No new changes since iteration 3'. State file is COMPLETELY unchanged (iteration 3, max_iterations 3, head_sha unchanged). Copilot is NOT invoked.

assertions.name: no_new_changes_message
assertions.description: stderr contains 'No new changes since iteration 3'

assertions.name: exit_code_one
assertions.description: exit code is 1

assertions.name: state_completely_unchanged
assertions.description: .claude/code-review.local.md is byte-identical to the pre-run state (iteration 3, max_iterations 3, head_sha unchanged)

assertions.name: no_copilot_invocation
assertions.description: copilot.js is not spawned
